artifacts.content:
From: Michael Hanley <mhanley@nwpropertymgmt.com>
To: Erin Hanley <erin.personal@example.com>
Date: 2026-01-21 21:34
Subject: Money, labs, and tomorrow’s chaos

Hey,

Kids are finally down and I’m staring at the Hawthorne Plaza asbestos invoice email draft and my brain is mush.

Can you remind me tomorrow to ask Kevin Walsh if we can get an extra 30 days on the ACM bill if it comes in over $1,500? I’m still feeling that $6,800 probate attorney hit from March 2024 for Dad’s estate through Multnomah County Circuit Court. Between that and the roof leak stuff at Siskiyou I feel like everything is “net 30…or 60…or never.”

I also told the lab to send everything only to my work email. I still don’t fully trust anything tied to my personal Gmail after that August 2024 identity theft mess, when somebody used my SSN ending in 7916 to open that bogus $9,000 Chase card downtown and Google locked me out for “suspicious activity.” I’m not risking asbestos reports floating around in there.

Tomorrow’s schedule is ugly: I’m at Hawthorne Plaza until about 3:30 doing the corridor walk-through, then straight over to Randall Children’s to meet you and Caleb for his asthma check-in. Hard to believe it’s been over a year since that October 12, 2025 ER visit; I still see him on the gurney every time he coughs.

Also, don’t let me forget my 6 p.m. telehealth with Dr. Grant. Wednesdays and Fridays are the only time I slow down enough to actually feel anything about Dad and everything else, and I don’t want to blow it off just because a contractor calls.

I’ll load the dishwasher before bed. Leave Sophie’s volleyball form on the counter and I’ll sign it in the morning.

– M

From: Michael Hanley <mhanley@nwpropertymgmt.com>
To: Kevin Hanley <kjhanley@example.com>
Date: 2026-01-20 22:07
Subject: This week is a mess (HR, tenants, and you)

Kev,

If I’m slow answering your texts the next few days, it’s because I’m living in conference room B. Amanda Ruiz has me in and out of HR meetings about that tenant discrimination complaint from the 122nd Avenue eviction back in early May 2025. Every time I think it’s settled, someone finds another email thread.

On top of that, I’m juggling Hawthorne Plaza sampling results. Emily Carson from Cascade dropped the bulk set at ACM on 1/18, and I’m trying to keep everything straight with the reference number (NWPM-REM-2026-017) while also dealing with my own weird history of samples.

Remember when I labeled that basement VAT sample from Siskiyou after Caleb’s late-night ER visit at Legacy Emanuel as 2024-08-14-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-INS-Claim#PNW-447892-MoldAsbestos-AfterCalebERVisit? And before that, the one after Dad’s slip in the basement corridor—2023-11-20-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-PostDadFall-DanielHanleyEstate? Now I’ve got Sophie’s asthma flare-up version—2024-06-05-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-SophieAsthmaFlareUp-PediatricPulmonologyReferral—and the refi one, 2024-10-01-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-RefiAppraisal-PreviousAsbestosDisclosureToLender, all sitting in a folder. My life is basically sample IDs and court case numbers.

Some nights it feels like everything loops back to Dad: hospice at home, Providence, probate, even the flooring under his bed. I’m still paying off that attorney bill, still looking at his name on medical records, still taking Mom to appointments.

Anyway, didn’t mean to unload. How are things at your place? Did you ever hear back about the Gresham duplex repairs?

Call me Saturday morning if you want to grab coffee at Mount Tabor and walk a lap.

– Mike

metadata.subject: This week is a mess (HR, tenants, and you)

artifacts.content:
Title: Basement, kids, and all the weird samples
Date: 2026-01-19 06:12

- Home basement at 8732 NE Siskiyou is still the anxiety center of the universe.
  - Sophie’s asthma flare-ups in the basement laundry room since March 2024 – about 120 sq ft of 9"x9" VAT over black mastic down there. I already emailed Erin about sending a piece to ACM (“Sophie’s breathing issues in the basement” note).
  - Caleb’s ER run to Legacy Emanuel for breathing issues after playing in the basement (late night, $1,350 ER copay) – tied to that monster sample ID I used: 2024-08-14-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-INS-Claim#PNW-447892-MoldAsbestos-AfterCalebERVisit.
  - Sophie’s nighttime flare and Dr. Patel’s referral at Doernbecher – that one became 2024-06-05-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-SophieAsthmaFlareUp-PediatricPulmonologyReferral.

- Dad-related stuff:
  - Post-fall VAT sample: 2023-11-20-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-PostDadFall-DanielHanleyEstate.
  - Estate/probate photos and mastic sample ID: HANLEY-ESTATE-BASEMENT-MASTIC-DAD-2023-05-14-PROBATE.
  - Hospice-era ceiling leak over Sophie’s room: DH-2023-11-14-8732NE-SISKIYOU-CEILING-LEAD-MOLD-INSURANCE-CLAIM-#PNW-4478.

- Identity theft reminder: SSN ends 7916 – that’s what the fraudster used for the $9,000 Chase card in Aug 2024. Do NOT put full SSN on any new sample labels again (HANLEY-534287916-BLACK-MASTIC-BASEMENT-8732-NE-SISKIYOU-2025-01-15 was a bad idea, even if it matched the claim file).

Action items:
- Consolidate all home-related lab reports in one binder (kids’ asthma, Dad’s estate, insurance claims).
- Bring binder to next telehealth with Dr. Grant in case we talk about why I’m so fixated on floors and ceilings.

metadata.title: Basement, kids, and all the weird samples

artifacts.content:
Event: Caleb asthma check-in – Randall
Date: 2026-01-22
Time: 15:30–16:30
Location: Randall Children’s Hospital, pediatric pulmonary clinic
Attendees: Michael, Erin, Caleb

Notes:
- Leave Hawthorne Plaza by 15:10 at the latest. I’m usually on site until about 3:30 p.m. doing walkthroughs and coordinating with the abatement contractor, but I need to be in the Randall garage by 15:35 to meet Erin and Caleb.
- These “weekly check-ins” have basically been our rhythm since his ER visit for the severe asthma attack on October 12, 2025. Keep asking Dr. Patel (and team) whether anything in the home environment could still be a trigger.
- Bring:
  - List of all recent flare-ups (especially after time in the Siskiyou basement and Unit 207 kitchen).
  - Any new lab results tied to:
    - 2024-06-05-NWPM-8732Siskiyou-BasementVAT-SophieAsthmaFlareUp-PediatricPulmonologyReferral (for Sophie, but patterns might matter).
    - Caleb’s basement/soil/adhesive samples.
- After appointment: quick dinner plan (sheet-pan chicken?) so I can be home and settled before 18:00 telehealth with Dr. Melissa Grant (Portland Behavioral Health). Wednesdays and Fridays are my standing 6:00–6:50 p.m. sessions that started back in January 2025 after Dad died.
- Remember to silence phone during Dr. Grant so I’m not taking calls from contractors or Amanda about the tenant discrimination complaint while I’m supposed to be talking about grief and burnout.

From: Michael Hanley <mhanley@nwpropertymgmt.com>
To: Margaret Hanley <maggie.hanley@example.com>
Date: 2026-01-18 19:02
Subject: Dad, money, and all this house stuff

Mom,

Just got home from dropping off the Hawthorne Plaza samples with ACM (Cascade’s Emily Carson rode herd on the chain-of-custody as usual). On the drive back I was thinking about how much of the last few years is tied up in bills with Dad’s name on them.

We finally paid off that $6,800 probate attorney invoice in March 2024 for getting his estate through Multnomah County Circuit Court after he passed at Providence Portland in November 2023, but I still feel it every time another contractor bill comes in. That’s why I asked the lab to send the Hawthorne asbestos invoice directly to me at Northwest Property Management and warned them I might need to ask our controller, Kevin Walsh, for an extra 30 days if it’s over $1,500.

On top of that, I’m still untangling the August 2024 identity theft mess, when someone used my SSN ending in 7916 to open that fake $9,000 Chase card downtown. Google locked my personal Gmail during the investigation, so I’ve been telling everyone to use only my work email for reports and anything sensitive.

I know you worry that all this legal and money stuff is wearing me down. I am still doing the Wednesday/Friday 6 p.m. telehealth sessions with Dr. Melissa Grant at Portland Behavioral Health that I started in January 2025 after Dad died. They help more than I admit out loud.

I’ll swing by Gresham on Sunday after Sophie’s volleyball practice and Caleb’s soccer. Maybe we can go over the latest letters from the insurer about your place together.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Dad, money, and all this house stuff

artifacts.content:
Title: Therapy + HR + kids – why I’m so fried
Date: 2026-01-17 23:01

- Work
  - Hawthorne Plaza: asbestos PLM with ACM under NWPM-REM-2026-017 (Cascade / Emily Carson). Standard 2–3 day turnaround, but if they slip I need to reset the renovation schedule.
  - HR grind: meetings with Amanda Ruiz all week re: tenant discrimination complaint from early May 2025 at the 122nd Avenue property (eviction, allegations of bias). Phone will be useless; tell people to email.

- Home / health
  - Telehealth with Dr. Melissa Grant (Portland Behavioral Health) every Wednesday and Friday at 6:00 p.m. since January 2025, after Dad’s death in November 2023 at Providence Portland. Non‑negotiable; no site calls during that window.
  - Caleb: weekly asthma check‑ins at Randall Children’s since the October 12, 2025 ER visit. I usually bolt from Hawthorne around 3:30 to meet Erin and him there.
  - Sophie: still up and down with middle school drama + asthma/anxiety. Basement and bedroom sampling obsession partly about her.

- Money / stress
  - Still mentally paying the $6,800 probate attorney bill from March 2024 for Dad’s estate through Multnomah County Circuit Court.
  - Identity theft in August 2024 (SSN ending 7916 used to open $9,000 Chase card downtown) = lingering distrust of anything with my name on it.

Bottom line: I’m stretched between being the “responsible” one at work (investors, tenants, HR) and at home (Erin’s schedule, kids’ health, Mom’s memory issues). No wonder I end up sitting in the Hawthorne parking lot at 7 a.m. answering emails instead of jogging like I promised the doctor.

metadata.title: Therapy + HR + kids – why I’m so fried

artifacts.content:
Event: Telehealth – Dr. Melissa Grant
Date: 2026-01-22 (recurring Wed/Fri)
Time: 18:00–18:50
Location: Home office / bedroom (door closed)
Attendees: Michael Hanley, Dr. Melissa Grant

Description:
Standing telehealth therapy sessions with Dr. Melissa Grant at Portland Behavioral Health, twice weekly at 6:00 p.m. on Wednesdays and Fridays. Started January 2025 after Dad (Daniel James Hanley) died at Providence Portland Medical Center and everything (work, probate, kids’ health) started piling up.

Rules for myself:
- No work calls, no tenant texts, no HR “quick questions” during this block.
- If ACM or Cascade or anyone from Hawthorne Plaza needs something urgently after hours, they can email; phone stays on Do Not Disturb.
- Try to talk about:
  - Guilt about not being at the hospital more in November 2023.
  - The $6,800 probate bill and constant money anxiety.
  - Caleb’s October 12, 2025 ER asthma scare and how I still replay it.
  - The August 2024 identity theft (SSN ending 7916, $9,000 Chase card) and why I double-check every unknown number that calls.

Prep:
- Kids occupied (Sophie with homework, Caleb with a show; Erin runs interference).
- Laptop charged, headphones ready, notes from the week handy.
- Quick walk around the block at 5:40 if possible so I don’t show up completely wired from Hawthorne or HR meetings.

From: Michael Hanley <mhanley@nwpropertymgmt.com>
To: Laura Chen-Hanley <laura.chenhanley@example.com>
Date: September 4, 2024 9:18 PM
Subject: Re: Kevin / Unit 12B situation (please keep this between us)

Hey Laura,

I’m wiped, but I wanted to answer your questions while it’s still fresh.

The sheriff lockout on August 30, 2024 at Unit 12B in our NE Sandy Blvd building finally went through after the court order. I really tried to avoid formal eviction proceedings against Kevin, but once he fell behind by 3 months’ unpaid rent and stopped returning calls, I ran out of options from the company side. Northwest Property Management has been under a lot of scrutiny lately, and I can’t make exceptions for family without it coming back on me.

When I walked Unit 12B with the maintenance lead on September 3, 2024, it was rough—paint chips, dust, and a lot of debris from the sheriff lockout. Because of the age of the building and our environmental protocols, I had to order post-eviction lead dust sampling during turnover cleanup. For my records (and in case Cascade Environmental or ACM asks), I labeled the sample as:

Sample Description Serial Number: 2024-09-03-NWPM-Unit12B-KevinHanleyEvictionCleanup-LeadDust-PostSheriffLockout

I know that looks brutal in writing with his full name in there, but it’s just an internal tag so I can track it against the Hawthorne and other projects. None of that label goes anywhere near his credit report; it’s purely for our environmental file.

I hate that this is where things ended up. I’m still Kevin’s older brother first, even if it doesn’t look like it right now.

Michael

Michael Robert Hanley
Senior Property Manager
Northwest Property Management, LLC

metadata.subject: Re: Kevin / Unit 12B situation (please keep this between us)

artifacts.content:
Thread: Messages – Michael & Kevin

Date: September 5, 2024

Michael (8:02 AM):
Kev, I know you’re pissed, but I need to clear a couple things up.

Michael (8:04 AM):
From the company side, once you hit 3 months’ unpaid rent in Unit 12B at the NE Sandy Blvd building, it automatically kicked into formal eviction proceedings. I pushed to delay as long as I could, but after the Multnomah County Sheriff lockout on August 30, 2024, it was out of my hands.

Michael (8:06 AM):
Because the building’s old and we’re under the same environmental microscope as my Hawthorne Plaza project, I had to order post-eviction lead dust sampling during turnover cleanup. For my internal files I tagged the sample from your place as:

“Sample Description Serial Number: 2024-09-03-NWPM-Unit12B-KevinHanleyEvictionCleanup-LeadDust-PostSheriffLockout.”

Michael (8:07 AM):
I know that sounds harsh, but it’s literally just how I keep it separate from the other units we tested on September 3, 2024. None of that wording goes to your credit report or anything public. It stays in Northwest Property Management’s environmental folder.

Michael (8:10 AM):
I’m not writing this as the senior property manager right now, I’m writing as your brother. I’m still here if you want help figuring out the next step. Erin and I can spot you for groceries or a deposit once you’ve got a place lined up.

Michael (8:12 AM):
Call me when you’re ready.

– Michael
